name: Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail
name_original: Ruta de los Misterios del Mar — circular
description: Hike the moderate 6.9-mile Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail in Asturias, famous for its dinosaur footprints and sea views.
region_intro: Hiking around Colunga, Asturias, offers diverse landscapes ranging from coastal paths to inland trails. The region is characterized by its dramatic coastline, featuring beaches known for ancient dinosaur footprints, and varied inland terrain that includes hillsides and wooded areas. Colunga is also a significant point along the historic Camino del Norte pilgrimage route, providing opportunities for both short and extended hikes. This mix of coastal and rural environments caters to various hiking preferences and fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Colunga The most popular hiking route is Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and passes by beaches known for dinosaur footprints. Another top favourite among local hikers is Northern Way (Asturias), a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This section of the historic pilgrimage route provides a mix of coastal scenery and inland stretches. Local hikers also love the Scenic Coastal Trail – Typical Asturian Granary loop from Colunga, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past traditional Asturian granaries, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Colunga is defined by coastal paths, varied inland terrain, and wooded areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Colunga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 180 reviews. Nearly 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Colunga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Colunga?
A: Colunga offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, from coastal paths with sea views to inland trails through wooded areas and traditional villages.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking in Colunga?
A: The trails in Colunga range from easy to moderate, with a few more challenging options. There are 14 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, 18 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more adventure, and 1 difficult route for experienced hikers. This variety ensures there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Colunga?
A: Yes, Colunga has several easy routes suitable for families. These often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances. While specific family-friendly routes aren't highlighted by name, the region's easy coastal walks and paths through charming villages are generally well-suited for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Colunga?
A: Many trails in Colunga are dog-friendly, especially the coastal paths and rural routes. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near protected natural spaces, and to check local regulations for specific beaches or nature reserves you might encounter.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Colunga?
A: Colunga is famous for its unique natural features. Along coastal trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048609624/arenal-de-moris-la-griega-beach-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can discover ancient dinosaur footprints on the beaches. Inland, you might encounter charming villages and varied landscapes. The region also features several beautiful waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1821428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molín de la Peña and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Colunga?
A: Yes, Colunga offers several circular routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Coastal Trail – Typical Asturian Granary loop from Colunga&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal areas and past traditional Asturian granaries. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Cantabrian Sea – La Beciella Beach loop from Santiago de Gobiendes&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning sea views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Colunga?
A: The best time for hiking in Colunga is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer offers warmer temperatures, ideal for coastal walks and beach visits, while winter can be cooler but still suitable for hiking, especially on clear days.

Q: Are there any routes that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Colunga is an important stop along the historic Camino del Norte (Northern Way). You can hike sections of this famous pilgrimage route, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047502658/northern-way-asturias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Northern Way (Asturias)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a mix of coastal scenery and inland stretches between towns.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Colunga?
A: The hiking routes in Colunga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from breathtaking coastal views to charming inland villages, and the unique experience of discovering dinosaur footprints.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Colunga's varied terrain includes several spots offering spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known viewpoint in the area, and you can also find stunning vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4535478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lastres from the San Roque viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic coastal scenery.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for hikes in Colunga?
A: Hikes in Colunga vary significantly in length and duration. Easy routes can be as short as a few kilometers, taking an hour or two. Moderate trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048609624/arenal-de-moris-la-griega-beach-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are around 11 km and take about 3 hours. Longer sections of the Camino del Norte can extend to over 16 km, requiring 4-5 hours or more.

Q: Are there any specific trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the Colunga area is home to several beautiful waterfalls. While not always directly on a main hiking route, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1821428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molín de la Peña and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails may pass near these natural attractions, offering a refreshing stop.
tips: After descending to La Griega beach, just before the sandbank, we can see dinosaur footprints. Jurassic footprints, which are easily identifiable thanks to an explanatory panel.
An interesting vestige of the Civil War, some old trenches dug on the coast by the Republican side, which were never used.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as moderate, but it is generally considered easy with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for most fitness levels and even children. The path is well-maintained and offers a comfortable walk along the coast.

faqs.question: Is the Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the coastal trails in Asturias, including this one, are generally dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near wildlife, and be mindful of other hikers and beach regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this coastal trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but the best times are spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild, and the crowds are smaller. Summer offers warmer temperatures for beach activities but can be busier. Winter can be beautiful but may bring more unpredictable weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Arenal de Morís - La Griega Beach Coastal Trail. It is located within the Paisaje Protegido de la Sierra del Sueve, but access to the trail itself is free and open to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking available near Arenal de Morís beach or La Griega beach, which serve as the start and end points of this linear trail. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked in these popular coastal locations.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers continuous views of the Cantabrian Sea and passes through diverse landscapes. Key highlights include the famous Dinosaur footprints in La Griega beach, the historic Isla Trenches, and the beautiful La Isla Beach. You'll also pass through charming villages and other picturesque beaches like La Beciella and El Barrigón.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route, or is it linear?
faqs.answer: This is a linear coastal trail, stretching approximately 11-12 kilometers one way between Arenal de Morís and La Griega Beach. If you plan to hike the full length, you'll need to arrange transport back to your starting point or hike it as an out-and-back.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route forms part of the larger Camino de Santiago de la Costa, adding a historical and cultural dimension to your hike. It also significantly overlaps with the 'Senda costera Arenal de Morís - Playa de La Griega' official trail.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to the trailheads at Arenal de Morís and La Griega Beach can be limited, especially outside of peak season. It's advisable to check local bus schedules (e.g., Alsa) in advance, particularly if you're relying on public transport for a one-way hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and engaging. You'll walk along sandy beaches, traverse sections of cliffs offering stunning sea views, and pass through peaceful eucalyptus forests. The path is generally well-defined, making for a pleasant walking experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Arenal de Morís and La Griega Beach, as well as the charming villages like Güerres/Huerres and San Xuan/San Juan de Duz along the route, offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These are great spots to refuel or stay overnight.

waypoints.name: Dinosaur footprints in La Griega beach
